description: Fix Vercel deployment by re-enabling static export and making page.tsx compatible with it. This eliminates all serverless functions (solving the 12-function Hobby plan limit) while keeping the /api/* rewrite to Render working.
steps: In apps/web/next.config.ts: Add `output: "export"` to the NextConfig object. Keep `images: { unoptimized: true }` as-is.

In apps/web/src/app/page.tsx: Remove `export const revalidate = 300` (incompatible with static export). Remove the `async function getPlans()` server-side fetch. Change `export default async function Home()` to a non-async function that uses FALLBACK_PLANS directly for the initial render. Add a new client component (either inline or in a separate file) that fetches plans from the API on mount and passes them to PlansGrid. The simplest approach: create a wrapper component `PlansSection` that is 'use client', fetches plans client-side via useEffect, and renders PlansGrid. Import and use it in page.tsx instead of the current server-side fetch pattern.

In apps/web/vercel.json: Ensure the rewrites configuration is correct for static export. The current config with source /api/:path* destination https://rupeerise-api.onrender.com/:path* should work with static sites on Vercel. No changes needed unless the build fails.
Run `npm install` in apps/web, then run `npx next build` in apps/web to verify the build succeeds and produces an `out/` directory.
Commit all changes with a descriptive message about fixing the Vercel deployment.
acceptance_criteria: apps/web/src/app/page.tsx has no `export const revalidate` and no server-side async fetch
Running `npx next build` in apps/web succeeds without errors
The build produces an `out/` directory with static HTML files
Plans are still displayed on the homepage (fetched client-side with fallback data for SSG)
verification: Run `npm install && npx next build` in apps/web directory - should complete successfully
Verify out/ directory exists after build: `ls apps/web/out/index.html`
Verify no revalidate export remains: `grep -r 'revalidate' apps/web/src/app/page.tsx` should return nothing
findings: Build succeeds with Next.js 16.2.6 Turbopack. All 27 pages generated as static HTML. The Counter component using require('next/dynamic') with ssr:false works fine with static export. No issues with vercel.json rewrites config.
